spaghetti-bolognese.name: Spaghetti Bolognese
spaghetti-bolognese.description: Classic Italian pasta with rich meat sauce

steps: Bring a large pot of generously salted water to a rolling boil for the Spaghetti.
Finely dice the Onion into small pieces for even cooking.
Mince the Garlic cloves - the finer the mince, the more flavor you'll release.
Heat the Olive Oil in a large, deep skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add the diced Onion and sauté for 4-5 minutes until softened and translucent.
Add the minced Garlic and cook for 30-60 seconds until fragrant - be careful not to burn it.
Add the Ground Beef, breaking it into small pieces with a wooden spoon. Season with Salt and Black Pepper.
Cook for 6-8 minutes until the beef is browned and no longer pink.
Stir in the Tomato Paste and cook for 2 minutes, coating the meat - this deepens the flavor.
Pour in the Canned Tomatoes, breaking them up with your spoon.
Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 25-30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
While the sauce simmers, cook the Spaghetti according to package directions until al dente.
Reserve 1 cup of pasta water, then drain the Spaghetti.
While pasta cooks, grate the Parmesan Cheese and set aside for serving.
If the sauce is too thick, stir in splashes of the reserved pasta water to reach your desired consistency.
Toss the drained Spaghetti directly into the sauce, mixing well to coat each strand.
Divide among warm bowls and top generously with the grated Parmesan Cheese.
Finish with a crack of fresh Black Pepper and serve immediately while hot.
tips: Reserve pasta water before draining - the starchy water helps sauce cling to the pasta and adds silkiness.
Don't overcrowd the beef when browning - work in batches if needed for better caramelization.
For extra depth, add a splash of red wine after browning the meat and let it reduce before adding tomatoes.
Fresh basil leaves make a beautiful and flavorful garnish if you have them on hand.

chicken-curry.name: Chicken Curry
chicken-curry.description: Aromatic and creamy Indian-style chicken curry

steps: Rinse the Rice under cold water until clear.
Combine with 450ml water in a pot, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-18 minutes.
While rice cooks, cut the Chicken Breast into 2cm bite-sized cubes, patting dry with paper towels for better browning.
Finely dice the Onion.
Mince the Garlic cloves.
Grate the fresh Ginger.
Measure out the Curry Powder, Cumin, and Turmeric into a small bowl.
Heat the Vegetable Oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add the diced Onion and cook for 6-8 minutes until golden brown and caramelized.
Add the minced Garlic and grated Ginger, stirring constantly for 30-60 seconds until very fragrant.
Add the Curry Powder, Cumin, and Turmeric. Stir for 30 seconds to toast the spices and release their oils.
Add the Chicken Breast pieces in a single layer. Let them sear undisturbed for 2 minutes, then stir and cook until no longer pink on the outside, about 3-4 minutes total.
Shake the can of Coconut Milk well, then pour it into the pan. Stir to combine and bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to medium-low and cook uncovered for 18-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the Chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Season with Salt to taste.
Fluff the Rice with a fork and divide among serving bowls.
Ladle the curry generously over the rice.
Garnish with fresh cilantro if desired and serve immediately.
tips: For richer flavor, use full-fat Coconut Milk rather than light versions.
Toasting the spices in oil before adding liquid releases their essential oils and intensifies the curry flavor.
If you like it spicier, add a diced fresh chili or a pinch of cayenne with the spices.
This curry tastes even better the next day as the flavors continue to meld together.

caesar-salad.name: Caesar Salad
caesar-salad.description: Crisp romaine lettuce with classic Caesar dressing and croutons

steps: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).
Mince the Garlic finely - half for the croutons, half for the dressing.
Cut the Bread into 2cm cubes.
Toss the bread cubes with half the Olive Oil and half the minced Garlic until evenly coated.
Spread the croutons in a single layer on a baking sheet.
Bake for 10-12 minutes, tossing halfway through, until golden brown and crispy. Let cool slightly.
While croutons bake, wash the Romaine Lettuce leaves thoroughly and dry completely in a salad spinner or with paper towels.
Make the dressing: whisk together the Mayonnaise, remaining Olive Oil, Lemon Juice, Dijon Mustard, remaining minced Garlic, Salt, and Black Pepper until smooth.
Use a vegetable peeler to shave thin ribbons of Parmesan Cheese.
Tear or chop the Romaine Lettuce into bite-sized pieces and place in a large salad bowl.
Drizzle the dressing over the lettuce and toss gently but thoroughly to coat every leaf.
Add the warm croutons and toss again briefly.
Top with generous shavings of Parmesan Cheese and a final crack of Black Pepper.
Serve immediately.
tips: Dry lettuce is crucial - dressing clings to dry leaves but slides off wet ones.
For extra flavor, rub your salad bowl with a cut garlic clove before adding the lettuce.
Add the croutons just before serving to keep them crispy.
For a more authentic Caesar, add a few anchovy fillets to the dressing and blend until smooth.

miso-honey-salmon.name: Miso Honey Glazed Salmon
miso-honey-salmon.description: Tender salmon fillets with a sweet and savory miso glaze

steps: Remove the Salmon Fillet from the refrigerator 20 minutes before cooking to bring to room temperature.
Rinse the Rice under cold water until clear.
Combine with 300ml water in a pot, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15-18 minutes.
Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Mince the Garlic cloves.
Grate the fresh Ginger.
Thinly slice the Green Onions on a bias, separating white and green parts.
Make the glaze: whisk together the White Miso Paste, Honey, Soy Sauce, Rice Vinegar, minced Garlic, grated Ginger, and Sesame Oil until smooth.
Pat the Salmon Fillet completely dry with paper towels.
Place skin-side down on the prepared baking sheet.
Reserve half the glaze, then brush the remaining half generously over the top and sides of the salmon.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until the salmon is just cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
Switch oven to broil. Brush the reserved glaze over the salmon and broil for 1-2 minutes, watching carefully, until the glaze is caramelized and slightly charred.
Fluff the cooked Rice and divide between serving bowls.
Place the glazed Salmon Fillet on top.
Sprinkle generously with Sesame Seeds and sliced Green Onions. Serve immediately.
tips: Don't skip bringing the salmon to room temperature - cold fish cooks unevenly and can end up dry on the outside before the center is done.
Watch the broiler carefully! The honey in the glaze can go from perfectly caramelized to burnt in seconds.
For extra umami, add a splash of mirin to the glaze if you have it.
This glaze also works beautifully on chicken thighs or pork tenderloin.

korean-beef-bowls.name: Korean Beef Bowls
korean-beef-bowls.description: Quick and flavorful ground beef with a sweet-spicy Korean sauce

steps: Rinse the Rice under cold water until clear.
Combine with water in a pot, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15-18 minutes.
While rice cooks, mince the Garlic finely.
Grate the fresh Ginger.
Whisk together the Soy Sauce, Brown Sugar, Sesame Oil, minced Garlic, grated Ginger, and Gochujang until the sugar dissolves.
Thinly slice the Green Onions, keeping white and green parts separate.
Slice the Cucumber into thin rounds.
Heat the Vegetable Oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add the Ground Beef in a single layer. Let it sear for 2 minutes without stirring for better browning.
Break up the beef with a wooden spoon and continue cooking for 4-5 minutes until no longer pink.
Drain excess fat if needed, leaving about 1 tablespoon in the pan.
Pour the prepared sauce over the beef and stir well to coat every piece.
Let the sauce simmer and reduce for 3-4 minutes until it thickens and glazes the meat.
Fluff the Rice with a fork and divide among serving bowls.
Spoon the saucy Korean beef over the rice.
Arrange the sliced Cucumber alongside.
Scatter the Green Onions and Sesame Seeds over the top. Serve immediately.
tips: Don't stir the beef immediately - letting it sear develops better flavor and texture.
Gochujang can vary in heat level, so taste the sauce and adjust to your preference.
For extra authenticity, serve with a fried egg on top - the runny yolk makes an amazing sauce.
Add a drizzle of extra Sesame Oil just before serving for enhanced aroma.

coconut-shrimp.name: Coconut Shrimp with Mango Salsa
coconut-shrimp.description: Crispy coconut-crusted shrimp with fresh tropical mango salsa

steps: Dice the Mango into 1cm cubes.
Finely dice the Red Onion and Jalapeno, removing seeds for less heat.
Roughly chop the Cilantro and juice the Lime.
Combine mango, red onion, jalapeno, cilantro, and lime juice in a bowl with a pinch of Salt. Refrigerate to chill while you prepare the shrimp.
Peel and devein the Large Shrimp if not already done, leaving tails on. Pat completely dry with paper towels.
Set up a breading station: seasoned Flour with Salt and Black Pepper in bowl 1, beaten Eggs in bowl 2, Shredded Coconut in bowl 3.
Bread the shrimp: holding by the tail, dredge in Flour (shake off excess), dip in Eggs (let excess drip off), then press firmly into Shredded Coconut. Place on a wire rack - don't stack.
Heat the Vegetable Oil in a deep pan to 350°F (175°C).
Fry shrimp in small batches of 4-5 for 2-3 minutes, turning once, until golden brown and cooked through. Don't overcrowd the pan.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate and season with a light sprinkle of Salt immediately.
Arrange the crispy coconut shrimp on a serving platter.
Place the mango salsa in a bowl alongside.
Garnish with extra Cilantro and Lime wedges. Serve immediately.
tips: The key to crispy shrimp is dry shrimp and hot oil - wet shrimp will make the breading soggy.
If you don't have a thermometer, test the oil by dropping in a small piece of coconut - it should sizzle immediately.
For extra crunch, add panko breadcrumbs to the coconut mixture.
The salsa can be made up to 4 hours ahead and refrigerated - the flavors will meld beautifully.

chicken-tikka-masala.name: Chicken Tikka Masala
chicken-tikka-masala.description: Tender chicken in a creamy spiced tomato sauce

steps: Cut the Chicken Breast into 3cm chunks.
Combine half the Garam Masala, half the Cumin, half the Paprika with the Yogurt in a bowl.
Add the chicken, mix well to coat, and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Rinse the Rice until water runs clear.
While chicken marinates, combine rice with water in a pot, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15-18 minutes.
Finely dice the Onion.
Mince the Garlic.
Grate the fresh Ginger.
Heat the Butter in a large, deep skillet over medium-high heat until foaming.
Remove the Chicken Breast from the marinade and cook in batches for 3-4 minutes per side until charred and caramelized - don't overcrowd. Set aside.
In the same pan, sauté the Onion for 5-6 minutes until softened and golden.
Add the minced Garlic, grated Ginger, and remaining Garam Masala, Cumin, and Paprika. Stir for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the Canned Tomatoes, breaking them up. Simmer for 15 minutes until reduced and thickened.
Reduce heat to low and stir in the Heavy Cream. Simmer gently for 2 minutes.
Return the charred Chicken to the sauce. Simmer for another 8-10 minutes until cooked through and the sauce is rich and creamy.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt.
Fluff the Rice and divide among bowls.
Spoon the Chicken Tikka Masala over the rice and garnish with fresh cilantro. Serve immediately.
tips: Marinating the chicken in Yogurt tenderizes it and helps the spices penetrate deeply.
The charred bits on the chicken add incredible flavor - don't skip this step.
For a richer sauce, add another tablespoon of Butter at the end and swirl until melted.
This dish tastes even better the next day once the flavors have melded.

beef-fajita-bowls.name: Beef Fajita Bowls
beef-fajita-bowls.description: Sizzling beef strips with peppers and onions over rice

steps: Rinse the Rice until water runs clear.
Combine with water in a pot, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15-18 minutes.
Slice the Beef Sirloin against the grain into thin 5mm strips.
Mix the Cumin, Chili Powder, salt, and pepper together, then toss the beef strips in the spice mixture.
Slice the Bell Peppers into thin strips.
Slice the Onion into thin half-moons.
Mince the Garlic.
Juice the Lime and roughly chop the Cilantro.
Heat a large cast-iron skillet with the Vegetable Oil over high heat until smoking.
Working in batches to avoid crowding, sear the seasoned Beef Sirloin strips for 1-2 minutes per side until nicely charred. Transfer to a plate.
In the same hot pan, add the Bell Peppers and Onion. Let them char without stirring for 1-2 minutes, then toss and cook for another 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp with good color.
Add the minced Garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return the beef to the pan, squeeze the Lime juice over everything, and toss to combine. Cook for 1 minute more.
Fluff the Rice and divide among serving bowls.
Pile the sizzling fajita beef and peppers over the rice.
Top with a generous dollop of Sour Cream.
Scatter fresh Cilantro over the top and serve with extra lime wedges.
tips: A screaming hot pan is essential for that authentic fajita char - don't be afraid of a little smoke.
Slicing beef against the grain is crucial for tender bites.
For extra flavor, add a splash of tequila or beer when the beef goes back in the pan.
Warm your bowls in the oven briefly before serving for restaurant-style presentation.

teriyaki-chicken.name: Teriyaki Chicken
teriyaki-chicken.description: Glazed chicken thighs with homemade teriyaki sauce

steps: Remove the Chicken Thighs from the refrigerator 20 minutes before cooking.
Pat dry with paper towels and score the skin lightly for crispier results.
Rinse the Rice until water runs clear. Combine with water, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15-18 minutes.
Mince the Garlic and grate the fresh Ginger.
Whisk together the Soy Sauce, Mirin, Brown Sugar, minced Garlic, and grated Ginger until the sugar dissolves.
Cut the Broccoli into bite-sized florets.
Thinly slice the Green Onions on a bias.
Place the Chicken Thighs skin-side down in a cold pan, then turn heat to medium. Cook without moving for 8-10 minutes until the skin is deeply golden and crispy.
Flip the chicken and cook for another 5-6 minutes until cooked through (internal temp 165°F/74°C).
While chicken finishes, steam the Broccoli florets for 3-4 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp.
Drain excess fat from the chicken pan, leaving about 1 tablespoon.
Pour the teriyaki sauce over the chicken and let it simmer for 2-3 minutes, spooning the sauce over the chicken until glossy and glazed.
Slice the glazed Chicken Thighs into strips.
Fluff the Rice and divide among serving bowls.
Arrange the sliced chicken and steamed Broccoli over the rice.
Drizzle any remaining sauce from the pan over the top.
Sprinkle with Sesame Seeds and sliced Green Onions.
tips: Starting chicken in a cold pan is the secret to restaurant-quality crispy skin.
Don't move the chicken while the skin crisps - let it render undisturbed.
If the sauce gets too thick, add a splash of water to loosen it up.
For extra umami, add a teaspoon of toasted sesame oil to the finished sauce.

mushroom-risotto.name: Creamy Mushroom Risotto
mushroom-risotto.description: Rich and creamy Italian rice with sautéed mushrooms

steps: Slice the Mixed Mushrooms into even pieces.
Finely dice the Onion.
Mince the Garlic.
Finely grate the Parmesan Cheese.
Heat the Chicken Stock in a saucepan and keep it warm over low heat throughout cooking - adding cold stock affects the texture.
Heat half the Butter in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add the Mixed Mushrooms and sauté for 5-6 minutes until golden brown. Season with salt and Thyme. Transfer to a plate.
In the same pan, heat the Olive Oil and remaining Butter over medium heat. Add the diced Onion and cook for 4-5 minutes until soft and translucent.
Add the minced Garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the Arborio Rice and stir for 2 minutes until the grains are toasted and slightly translucent at the edges.
Pour in the White Wine and stir until completely absorbed.

Add the warm Chicken Stock one ladle at a time (about 125ml), stirring frequently. Wait until each addition is almost absorbed before adding the next. Continue for 18-20 minutes until the rice is al dente - creamy on the outside with a slight bite.
Remove from heat and fold in the sautéed mushrooms, grated Parmesan Cheese, and a final knob of Butter.
Let the risotto rest for 1-2 minutes.
Divide among warm plates, top with extra Parmesan and a crack of black pepper, and serve immediately.
tips: Never rinse Arborio Rice - the starch is what makes risotto creamy.
Keep the stock warm - adding cold liquid shocks the rice and affects the final texture.
The stirring isn't about being constant, but about ensuring the rice doesn't stick and the starch releases evenly.
A proper risotto should spread slightly on the plate, not sit in a mound.

fish-tacos.name: Crispy Fish Tacos
fish-tacos.description: Beer-battered fish with tangy slaw in soft tortillas

steps: Make the chipotle slaw: finely shred the Cabbage, mix with Mayonnaise, half the Lime juice, and Chipotle. Season with salt and refrigerate - it gets better as it sits.
Make the batter: whisk the Flour with the Beer until smooth like thick cream. Season with a pinch of salt.
Cut the White Fish Fillets into strips about 3cm wide.
Pat completely dry with paper towels and season with salt.
Heat the Vegetable Oil in a deep pan to 375°F (190°C).
While oil heats, juice the remaining Lime and roughly chop the Cilantro.

Working in batches, dip the fish strips into the batter (let excess drip off), then carefully lower into the hot oil. Fry for 3-4 minutes, turning once, until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels and season with salt immediately.
Warm the Flour Tortillas in a dry skillet for 30 seconds per side until pliable and slightly charred.
Assemble: place crispy fish on each warm tortilla, top with chipotle slaw, sprinkle with Cilantro, and squeeze Lime juice over. Serve immediately with extra lime wedges.
tips: The key to crispy batter is cold beer - keep it refrigerated until you're ready to mix.
Don't overcrowd the oil - frying too many pieces at once drops the temperature and makes fish greasy.
If you don't have a thermometer, test the oil by dropping a tiny bit of batter in - it should sizzle immediately and float.
For extra crunch, add a tablespoon of cornstarch to the batter.

pork-stir-fry.name: Sweet and Spicy Pork Stir Fry
pork-stir-fry.description: Tender pork with vegetables in a sticky sweet-spicy sauce

steps: Rinse the Rice until water runs clear.
Combine with water in a pot, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15-18 minutes.
Slice the Pork Tenderloin against the grain into thin 5mm strips.
Toss the pork strips with the Cornstarch until evenly coated.
Slice the Bell Peppers into thin strips.
Trim the ends off the Snap Peas.
Mince the Garlic finely.
Whisk together the Soy Sauce, Honey, and Sriracha in a small bowl.
Heat half the Vegetable Oil in a wok over high heat until smoking.
Add the cornstarch-coated Pork Tenderloin in a single layer. Sear undisturbed for 1 minute, then stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until browned and just cooked through. Transfer to a plate.
Add the remaining oil to the wok. Add the Bell Peppers and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add the Snap Peas and stir-fry for another 1-2 minutes until crisp-tender with a bit of char.
Add the minced Garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return the pork to the wok, pour in the sauce, and toss everything together for 1 minute until the sauce is glossy and coats everything.
Fluff the Rice and divide among serving bowls.
Spoon the pork stir-fry over the rice.
Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced green onions. Serve immediately.
tips: The cornstarch coating is key - it creates a protective barrier that keeps the pork juicy and gives the sauce something to cling to.
A smoking hot wok is essential for proper stir-frying - don't be afraid of high heat.
Work in batches if needed - overcrowding the wok will steam the meat instead of searing it.
Adjust the Sriracha to your heat preference - start with less and add more at the end if needed.

greek-chicken-bowls.name: Greek Chicken Bowls
greek-chicken-bowls.description: Mediterranean chicken with cucumber, tomatoes, and tzatziki

steps: Cut the Chicken Breast into 3cm chunks.
Whisk together half the Olive Oil, juice from half the Lemon, the Oregano, and one minced Garlic clove. Add the chicken and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Grate half the Cucumber and squeeze out excess moisture. Mix with the Greek Yogurt, remaining minced Garlic, remaining Lemon juice, and a pinch of salt. Refrigerate until serving.
Rinse the Rice until water runs clear.
Combine with water, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15-18 minutes.
While rice cooks, dice the remaining Cucumber into 1cm cubes.
Halve the Cherry Tomatoes.
Thinly slice the Red Onion.
Crumble the Feta Cheese.
Heat the remaining Olive Oil in a grill pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Remove the Chicken Breast from the marinade and cook for 5-6 minutes per side until nicely charred and cooked through (internal temp 165°F/74°C).
Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes, then slice into strips.
Fluff the Rice and divide among serving bowls.
Arrange the sliced grilled chicken on one side. Add the diced Cucumber, halved Cherry Tomatoes, and sliced Red Onion in sections.
Crumble the Feta Cheese generously over the top.
Add a generous dollop of tzatziki.
Drizzle with extra Olive Oil and a sprinkle of Oregano.
tips: Marinating the chicken in lemon and oregano is essential for authentic Greek flavor.
Squeezing moisture from the grated cucumber prevents watery tzatziki.
Let the chicken rest before slicing to keep it juicy.
For extra authenticity, add kalamata olives and a sprinkle of sumac.

shrimp-scampi.name: Garlic Shrimp Scampi
shrimp-scampi.description: Succulent shrimp in a buttery garlic white wine sauce

steps: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to boil for the Linguine.
While water heats, peel and devein the Large Shrimp if not already done.
Pat the shrimp completely dry with paper towels.
Mince the Garlic finely.
Zest the Lemon, then juice it, keeping zest and juice separate.
Roughly chop the fresh Parsley.
Cook the Linguine according to package directions until al dente.
Reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking water before draining.
While pasta cooks, heat the Olive Oil and half the Butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the Large Shrimp in a single layer and cook for 1-2 minutes per side until pink and just cooked through. Transfer to a plate immediately.
Reduce heat to medium. Add the remaining Butter to the pan.
Add the minced Garlic and Red Pepper Flakes, stirring for 30 seconds until fragrant - don't let the garlic brown.
Pour in the White Wine and Lemon juice. Let it bubble and reduce by half, about 2 minutes.
Add the drained Linguine to the pan and toss to coat. Add splashes of reserved pasta water as needed to create a silky sauce.
Return the shrimp to the pan and toss gently to combine.
Remove from heat and stir in the Parsley and Lemon zest.
Divide among warm bowls. Finish with extra Red Pepper Flakes for heat. Serve immediately.
tips: Reserve pasta water BEFORE draining - you can't get it back, and it's essential for the sauce.
Don't overcook the shrimp - they continue cooking from residual heat when added back to the pasta.
The wine should be something you'd drink - cooking wine has poor flavor.
A splash of extra butter at the very end adds restaurant-quality richness.

bbq-chicken.name: BBQ Glazed Chicken
bbq-chicken.description: Juicy chicken with smoky homemade BBQ glaze

steps: Remove the Chicken Thighs from the refrigerator 20 minutes before cooking.
Season generously on both sides with salt and pepper.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).

Make the BBQ sauce: in a small saucepan, whisk together the Ketchup, Brown Sugar, Apple Cider Vinegar, Worcestershire Sauce, Smoked Paprika, and Garlic Powder. Bring to a simmer over medium heat and cook for 5 minutes until slightly thickened. Set aside.
Place the seasoned Chicken Thighs on a baking sheet skin-side up and bake for 25 minutes.
While chicken bakes, shuck the Corn on the Cob and remove all silk.
Make the coleslaw by mixing the Coleslaw Mix with Mayonnaise, salt, pepper, and a splash of Apple Cider Vinegar.
After 25 minutes, brush the chicken generously with BBQ sauce. Return to oven for another 10 minutes until glazed and internal temp reaches 165°F (74°C).
Grill or boil the Corn on the Cob for 8-10 minutes until tender and slightly charred.
Brush the Chicken Thighs with another layer of BBQ sauce while hot.
Serve the glazed chicken alongside the corn and coleslaw with extra BBQ sauce on the side.
tips: Don't add the BBQ sauce too early - the sugars will burn before the chicken is cooked through.
For smokier flavor, add a teaspoon of liquid smoke to the sauce.
Let the corn rest for 2 minutes after grilling, then brush with butter.
For crispy chicken skin, broil for the last 2 minutes of cooking.

vegetable-pad-thai.name: Vegetable Pad Thai
vegetable-pad-thai.description: Classic Thai noodles with vegetables and tangy sauce

steps: Soak the Rice Noodles in warm (not boiling) water for 30 minutes until pliable but still slightly firm.
At the same time, press the Tofu: wrap in paper towels, place a heavy object on top, and let drain for 15 minutes.
Cut the pressed Tofu into 2cm cubes.
While noodles soak, slice the Green Onions separating whites and greens.
Roughly crush the Peanuts.
Juice the Lime.
Make the pad thai sauce: whisk together the Tamarind Paste, Fish Sauce, and Brown Sugar until the sugar dissolves.
Heat 2 tablespoons of Vegetable Oil in a wok over high heat until smoking.
Add the Tofu cubes and fry without stirring for 2 minutes until golden on the bottom. Flip and cook another 2 minutes. Transfer to a plate.
Add remaining oil to the wok. Crack in the Eggs, let them set for 30 seconds, then scramble into large pieces. Push to the side of the wok.
Add the drained Rice Noodles and the pad thai sauce. Toss vigorously with tongs for 2-3 minutes until noodles are coated and softened.
Add half the Bean Sprouts and the white parts of the Green Onions. Toss for another minute.
Return the tofu and toss everything together.
Divide among plates.
Top with remaining fresh Bean Sprouts, crushed Peanuts, and sliced Green Onion greens.
Serve with Lime wedges, extra Fish Sauce, or chili flakes to taste.
tips: Don't over-soak the noodles - they should be pliable but not mushy. They finish cooking in the wok.
A smoking hot wok is essential for that authentic 'wok hei' (breath of the wok) flavor.
Adding some bean sprouts raw at the end provides textural contrast.
For a vegan version, use soy sauce instead of fish sauce and skip the eggs.

lamb-kofta.name: Lamb Kofta with Mint Yogurt
lamb-kofta.description: Spiced lamb skewers with cool mint yogurt sauce

steps: If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes to prevent burning.
Grate the Onion on the large holes of a box grater - this creates a paste that binds the meat.
Mince the Garlic finely.
Finely chop the Fresh Mint, reserving some whole leaves for garnish.
In a large bowl, combine the Ground Lamb with the grated Onion, minced Garlic, Cumin, Coriander, 1 tsp salt, and a good crack of pepper. Mix thoroughly with your hands.
Make the mint yogurt: mix the Greek Yogurt with most of the chopped Fresh Mint and a pinch of salt. Refrigerate until serving.
Slice the Cucumber into rounds.
Halve the Cherry Tomatoes.
Divide the lamb mixture into 8 portions. Wet your hands and form each portion around a skewer into an oval sausage shape about 15cm long.
Preheat grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Grill the kofta for 4-5 minutes per side, turning carefully, until charred outside and cooked through (internal temp 160°F/71°C).
Warm the Pita Bread on the grill for 30 seconds per side until soft and pliable.
Slide the kofta off skewers into warm Pita Bread.
Add sliced Cucumber and halved Cherry Tomatoes.
Drizzle generously with mint yogurt.
Scatter with reserved Fresh Mint leaves. Serve immediately.
tips: Grating the onion rather than chopping ensures even distribution and helps bind the meat.
Wet hands prevent the meat from sticking while forming kofta.
Don't press or flatten the kofta while grilling - let them cook undisturbed for proper browning.
For extra flavor, add a pinch of cinnamon or sumac to the lamb mixture.

cajun-shrimp-pasta.name: Cajun Shrimp Pasta
cajun-shrimp-pasta.description: Creamy pasta with spicy Cajun-seasoned shrimp

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil for the pasta.
While water heats, peel and devein the Large Shrimp and pat dry with paper towels.
Toss the shrimp with half the Cajun Seasoning.
Dice the Bell Pepper into small pieces.
Mince the Garlic finely.
Grate the Parmesan Cheese.
Roughly chop the fresh Parsley.
Cook the Penne Pasta according to package directions until al dente.
Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
While pasta cooks, heat the Butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the seasoned Large Shrimp and cook for 2-3 minutes per side until pink and slightly charred. Transfer to a plate.
In the same pan, add the diced Bell Pepper and remaining Cajun Seasoning. Cook for 3-4 minutes until the pepper is softened.
Add the minced Garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the Heavy Cream and bring to a simmer. Cook for 3-4 minutes until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Add the drained pasta and toss to coat, adding splashes of pasta water if the sauce is too thick.
Stir in the grated Parmesan Cheese until melted.
Return the shrimp to the pan and toss to combine.
Divide among warm bowls. Garnish with fresh Parsley and extra Parmesan. Serve immediately.
tips: Cajun seasoning varies by brand - taste before adding more, some are much spicier than others.
Don't overcook the shrimp initially - they'll finish cooking when tossed back into the sauce.
The starchy pasta water is essential for a silky, clingy sauce.
For extra richness, add a splash of white wine when cooking the bell pepper.

honey-garlic-pork.name: Honey Garlic Pork Chops
honey-garlic-pork.description: Pan-seared pork chops with sticky honey garlic glaze

steps: Remove the Pork Chops from the refrigerator 20 minutes before cooking to bring to room temperature.
If making fresh Mashed Potatoes, peel and quarter the potatoes and put them on to boil in salted water.
Pat the Pork Chops completely dry with paper towels and season generously with salt and pepper.
Mince the Garlic finely.
Trim the ends off the Green Beans.
Heat the Olive Oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add the Pork Chops and sear without moving for 4 minutes until a golden crust forms. Flip and cook another 4 minutes. Transfer to a plate to rest.
Reduce heat to medium. Add the Butter to the same pan.
Add the minced Garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant and just starting to turn golden.
Add the Honey, Soy Sauce, and Thyme. Stir and let it bubble for 2 minutes until slightly thickened into a glaze.
Return the Pork Chops to the pan and spoon the glaze over them repeatedly, cooking for another 2 minutes to coat and finish cooking through.
Steam or blanch the Green Beans for 3-4 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp.
Drain and mash the potatoes with butter and cream until smooth (or heat prepared Mashed Potatoes).
Create a bed of Mashed Potatoes on each plate.
Place a glazed Pork Chop on top, spooning extra glaze from the pan over it.
Arrange the Green Beans alongside.
tips: The key to the glaze is letting the honey caramelize without burning - watch the heat.
Resting the pork before returning it to the glaze keeps it juicy.
For thicker pork chops, finish them in a 400°F oven after searing.
Add a splash of apple cider vinegar at the end to cut the sweetness and add brightness.

tuscan-chicken.name: Creamy Tuscan Chicken
tuscan-chicken.description: Chicken in a sun-dried tomato and spinach cream sauce

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil for the Pasta.
While water heats, slice the Chicken Breast horizontally to create two thinner cutlets.
Season the cutlets on both sides with salt, pepper, and half the Italian Seasoning.
Slice the Sun-dried Tomatoes into strips.
Mince the Garlic finely.
Grate the Parmesan Cheese.
Cook the Pasta according to package directions until al dente.
Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
While pasta cooks, heat the Olive Oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the Chicken Breast cutlets and cook for 4-5 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through (internal temp 165°F/74°C). Transfer to a plate and let rest.
In the same pan, add the minced Garlic and remaining Italian Seasoning. Cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the Sun-dried Tomatoes and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the Heavy Cream and bring to a gentle simmer. Cook for 3-4 minutes until slightly thickened.
Add the Spinach in handfuls, stirring until each batch wilts before adding more.
Stir in the grated Parmesan Cheese until melted and smooth.
Slice the rested chicken and return it to the sauce, spooning the creamy mixture over the chicken.
Divide the drained Pasta among plates.
Top with the creamy Tuscan chicken and plenty of sauce.
Garnish with additional Parmesan and a crack of black pepper.
tips: Butterflying or pounding the chicken ensures even cooking and more surface area for the sauce.
Sun-dried tomatoes packed in oil have more flavor than dry-packed ones.
The spinach will seem like too much at first but wilts down significantly.
A splash of the sun-dried tomato oil adds extra depth to the sauce.

beef-bulgogi.name: Beef Bulgogi
beef-bulgogi.description: Korean marinated beef with sweet and savory flavors

steps: Place the Beef Ribeye in the freezer for 30 minutes - this makes it much easier to slice paper-thin.
Core and grate the Asian Pear.
Mince the Garlic and grate the fresh Ginger.
Thinly slice the Green Onions on a bias.
Rinse the Rice until water runs clear.
Slice the chilled Beef Ribeye against the grain as thinly as possible (2-3mm).
Make the marinade: whisk together the grated Asian Pear, Soy Sauce, Sesame Oil, Brown Sugar, minced Garlic, and grated Ginger until smooth.
Coat the beef slices in the marinade.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour (or overnight for best results).
Combine the Rice with water in a pot, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15-18 minutes.
Heat a large cast-iron skillet or grill pan over high heat until smoking.
Working in batches to avoid overcrowding, add the marinated beef in a single layer and sear without moving for 1-2 minutes until caramelized.
Flip and cook another 1-2 minutes.
Transfer to a plate - residual heat will finish cooking the thin slices.
Fluff the Rice and divide among serving bowls.
Pile the caramelized Beef Bulgogi over the rice.
Scatter generously with sliced Green Onions and Sesame Seeds.
Serve with kimchi and lettuce leaves for wrapping.
tips: The Asian Pear contains enzymes that naturally tenderize the beef - don't skip this step.
Paper-thin slices are essential - if your butcher can slice it for you, even better.
A screaming hot pan is key for that caramelized, slightly charred exterior.
Bulgogi is traditionally wrapped in lettuce with rice and condiments for a perfect bite.

lemon-herb-chicken.name: Lemon Herb Roasted Chicken
lemon-herb-chicken.description: Juicy roasted chicken with bright lemon and fresh herbs

steps: Remove the Chicken Legs from the refrigerator 30 minutes before cooking.
Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
Zest both Lemons, then juice them.
Mince or thinly slice the Garlic cloves.
Strip the Rosemary leaves from stems and roughly chop.
Strip the Thyme leaves.
Quarter the Potatoes into even pieces.
Trim the woody ends from the Asparagus.
Whisk together most of the Olive Oil, Lemon zest, half the Lemon juice, the Rosemary, Thyme, and minced Garlic.
Season with salt and pepper.
Rub the herb mixture generously all over the Chicken Legs, getting under the skin where possible.
Toss the Potatoes with the remaining Olive Oil, salt, and pepper.
Arrange the Potatoes in a single layer on a large baking sheet.
Nestle the chicken legs among the potatoes.
Roast for 35 minutes, basting the chicken with pan juices halfway through.
Add the Asparagus around the edges and drizzle with a little oil.
Roast for another 10 minutes until the chicken skin is crispy and golden (internal temp 165°F/74°C), potatoes are tender, and asparagus is bright green.
Let the Chicken Legs rest for 5 minutes.
Arrange on a platter with the roasted Potatoes and Asparagus.
Drizzle the remaining Lemon juice and all pan juices over everything.
tips: Getting the herb butter under the skin keeps the chicken incredibly moist.
Cut potatoes evenly so they cook at the same rate.
Don't skip the resting time - cutting too early lets all the juices run out.
Save the pan drippings - they're liquid gold for the final drizzle.

thai-basil-chicken.name: Thai Basil Chicken
thai-basil-chicken.description: Aromatic stir-fried chicken with Thai basil and chilies

steps: Rinse the Jasmine Rice until water runs clear.
Combine with water in a pot, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 12-15 minutes.
Thinly slice the Thai Chilies (remove seeds for less heat).
Roughly chop the Garlic.
Pick the Thai Basil leaves from the stems.
Mix the Soy Sauce, Oyster Sauce, Fish Sauce, and Sugar until the sugar dissolves.
Heat the Vegetable Oil in a wok over high heat until smoking.
Add the sliced Thai Chilies and chopped Garlic and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the Ground Chicken and break it up with a spatula.
Let it sear without stirring for 1 minute to develop color, then continue stir-frying for 3-4 minutes until cooked through with crispy edges.
Pour in the sauce mixture and toss to coat.
Let it bubble for 1-2 minutes until the sauce reduces slightly and glazes the chicken.
Remove from heat immediately and fold in the Thai Basil leaves - they should just wilt from the residual heat.
Divide the fluffy Jasmine Rice among serving plates.
Spoon the Thai basil chicken over the rice.
Top with a fried egg if desired and serve immediately while the basil is still bright and aromatic.
tips: Don't cook the basil - it should be added off heat and just wilted. Cooking turns it black and bitter.
Thai holy basil (bai gaprao) is traditional, but Thai sweet basil works well too.
The sauce should reduce to a coating consistency, not pooling at the bottom.
For authentic presentation, serve with a fried egg on top - the runny yolk becomes part of the sauce.

salmon-teriyaki-bowl.name: Salmon Teriyaki Bowl
salmon-teriyaki-bowl.description: Glazed salmon over rice with edamame and pickled ginger

steps: Remove the Salmon Fillet from the refrigerator 20 minutes before cooking.
Rinse the Sushi Rice until water runs clear.
Cook the rice according to package directions (typically 1:1.1 rice to water, bring to boil, reduce heat, cover 15 minutes, then rest 10 minutes off heat).
Make the teriyaki sauce: whisk together the Soy Sauce, Mirin, and Honey.
Pat the Salmon Fillet completely dry with paper towels.
Cut the Nori into thin strips with scissors.
Cook the Edamame in salted boiling water for 3-4 minutes until tender.
Drain and shell the edamame if needed.
Slice the Avocado and drizzle with a little lemon or lime juice to prevent browning.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat (no oil needed for skin-on salmon).
Place the Salmon Fillet flesh-side down and sear for 3 minutes until a nice crust forms.
Flip to skin-side down and cook another 3 minutes until the skin is crispy.
Pour the teriyaki sauce over the salmon and let it bubble and reduce for 1-2 minutes, spooning the glaze over the fish.
Divide the Sushi Rice between bowls.
Place the glazed Salmon Fillet on top.
Arrange the sliced Avocado, Edamame, and Pickled Ginger in sections around the bowl.
Sprinkle with Sesame Seeds and Nori strips.
Drizzle remaining teriyaki sauce over the top.
tips: Good sushi rice should be slightly sticky but not mushy - don't skip the rinsing step.
For the best texture, cook salmon to medium rather than well-done - it should flake but still be moist.
Fan the rice as it cools slightly for the traditional glossy finish.
Add a drizzle of sriracha mayo for extra flavor if desired.

moroccan-chicken.name: Moroccan Spiced Chicken
moroccan-chicken.description: Aromatic chicken with preserved lemons and olives

steps: Remove the Chicken Thighs from the refrigerator 30 minutes before cooking.
Mix the Cumin and Cinnamon with salt and pepper.
Rub the spice mixture generously over the chicken on all sides.
Bloom the Saffron in 2 tablespoons of warm Chicken Stock.
Rinse the Preserved Lemons, discard the pulp, and slice the rind into thin strips.
Thinly slice the Onion.
Mince the Garlic.
Roughly chop the fresh Cilantro.
Heat oil in a tagine or Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Brown the Chicken Thighs skin-side down for 4-5 minutes until golden.
Flip and brown the other side.
Transfer the chicken to a plate.
Sauté the sliced Onion in the same pot for 5-6 minutes until softened and lightly golden.
Add the minced Garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the remaining Chicken Stock and the saffron-infused liquid.
Scrape up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the Chicken Thighs to the pot.
Add the sliced Preserved Lemons and Green Olives, tucking them around the chicken.
Cover and simmer on low for 30-35 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
About 5 minutes before the chicken is done, pour boiling stock or water over the Couscous (1:1 ratio) and cover.
Let the couscous stand for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork.
Spread the Couscous on a serving platter.
Arrange the Chicken Thighs with the Preserved Lemons and Green Olives on top.
Spoon the aromatic sauce generously over everything.
Scatter with fresh Cilantro and serve family-style.
tips: Preserved lemons are salty, so go easy on additional salt.
If you don't have a tagine, a Dutch oven works perfectly and creates the same braised effect.
The saffron is expensive but essential for authentic Moroccan flavor - a little goes a long way.
This dish is even better the next day after the flavors have melded.

black-bean-tacos.name: Spicy Black Bean Tacos
black-bean-tacos.description: Hearty vegetarian tacos with seasoned black beans

steps: Drain and rinse the Black Beans.
Finely dice the Red Onion and place in a small bowl with a squeeze of Lime juice to pickle slightly - do this first so it pickles while you work.
Crumble the Queso Fresco.
Roughly chop the fresh Cilantro.
Juice the remaining Lime.
Cut the Avocado in half and remove the pit.
Mash the flesh with Lime juice and a pinch of salt, keeping it chunky for better texture.
Place half the Black Beans in a bowl and mash with a fork until mostly smooth but still textured.
Combine the mashed and whole beans in a saucepan.
Add the Cumin, Chipotle, juice of one Lime, and a pinch of salt.
Warm over medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring, until heated through and slightly thickened.
Char the Corn Tortillas: hold each over an open gas flame for 10-15 seconds per side until lightly charred, or heat in a dry skillet until pliable and spotted.
Lay out the charred tortillas (2 per person).
Spread guacamole on each tortilla.
Spoon warm Black Beans on top.
Add pickled Red Onion and crumbled Queso Fresco.
Drizzle with Salsa Verde and scatter with fresh Cilantro.
Serve with extra Lime wedges.
tips: Mashing half the beans creates a creamy base that holds the tacos together better.
Pickling the onion in lime juice tames its sharpness and adds brightness.
Double-stack the tortillas if they're thin - it prevents tearing and adds more corn flavor.
For extra heat, add more chipotle or fresh jalapeño to the beans.

ginger-beef.name: Ginger Beef Stir Fry
ginger-beef.description: Crispy beef strips with fresh ginger and vegetables

steps: Rinse the Rice until water runs clear.
Combine with water in a pot, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15-18 minutes.
Slice the Beef Flank against the grain into thin 5mm strips.
Toss the beef strips with the Cornstarch until evenly coated - this is the secret to crispy beef.
Peel and julienne the Fresh Ginger into matchstick-sized pieces.
Mince the Garlic.
Slice the Bell Peppers into thin strips.
Mix the Soy Sauce, Rice Vinegar, and Brown Sugar until the sugar dissolves.
Heat half the Vegetable Oil in a wok over high heat until smoking.
Working in batches to avoid crowding, fry the cornstarch-coated Beef Flank strips for 2-3 minutes until crispy and golden.
Transfer the beef to a plate.
Add the remaining Vegetable Oil to the wok and stir-fry the Bell Peppers for 2 minutes until slightly charred but still crisp.
Add the julienned Fresh Ginger and minced Garlic and stir-fry for 30 seconds until very fragrant.
Return all the crispy beef to the wok.
Pour in the sauce and toss vigorously for 1 minute until the sauce glazes everything and becomes slightly sticky.
Fluff the Rice and divide among plates or bowls.
Pile the ginger beef and peppers over the rice.
Garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds and serve immediately while the beef is still crispy.
tips: The cornstarch coating must be even - shake off excess for crispier results.
A screaming hot wok is essential - the beef should sizzle immediately on contact.
Don't skip the ginger - it's the star of this dish. Fresh ginger is mandatory.
Serve immediately - the crispy coating will soften if it sits too long.

chicken-parmesan.name: Crispy Chicken Parmesan
chicken-parmesan.description: Breaded chicken cutlets with marinara and melted cheese

steps: Bring a large pot of generously salted water to a rolling boil for the Spaghetti.
Butterfly the Chicken Breast and pound to even 1cm thickness between plastic wrap.
Season Flour with salt and pepper in a shallow bowl.
Beat the Eggs in a second bowl.
Mix the Breadcrumbs with half the grated Parmesan Cheese in a third bowl.
Slice the Mozzarella and grate the remaining Parmesan.
Dredge each Chicken Breast cutlet in Flour (shake off excess), dip in Eggs, then press firmly into the Breadcrumb mixture on both sides.
Heat the Olive Oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Pan-fry the breaded chicken for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and crispy.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate.
Cook the Spaghetti in the boiling water until al dente.
Preheat the broiler.
Spoon Marinara Sauce into a baking dish.
Place the fried chicken on top of the sauce.
Spoon more Marinara over each piece, then layer with Mozzarella and remaining grated Parmesan.
Broil for 2-3 minutes until the cheese is bubbly, golden, and slightly charred in spots.
Divide the Spaghetti among plates and spoon extra Marinara Sauce over it.
Place a chicken parmesan cutlet on top of each portion.
Garnish with fresh basil and extra Parmesan and serve immediately while the cheese is still melty.
tips: Pounding the chicken ensures even cooking and prevents the outside from burning before the inside is done.
The breading station order (flour-egg-crumb) is important - flour helps the egg stick, egg helps the crumbs stick.
Don't move the chicken while the first side is browning - a good crust needs undisturbed contact with the pan.
For extra crispy breading, add grated parmesan directly to the breadcrumbs.

coconut-curry-soup.name: Coconut Curry Soup
coconut-curry-soup.description: Warming Thai-inspired soup with vegetables and rice noodles

steps: Wrap the Tofu in paper towels, place a heavy object on top, and let drain for 15 minutes.
Soak the Rice Noodles in warm water for 20 minutes until pliable but not fully soft.
Slice the Mushrooms into thin strips.
Trim the Bok Choy and quarter lengthwise.
Juice the Lime.
Roughly chop the fresh Cilantro.
Cut the pressed Tofu into 2cm cubes.
Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.
Fry the Tofu cubes for 2-3 minutes per side until golden.
Transfer the tofu to a plate.
Add the Red Curry Paste to the same pot and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant and the color deepens.
Pour in the Coconut Milk and Vegetable Stock, stirring to combine.
Bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the sliced Mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Drain the Rice Noodles and add them to the pot along with the Bok Choy.
Simmer for 3-4 minutes until the noodles are tender and the bok choy is wilted but still bright green.
Season with Fish Sauce and Lime juice to taste.
Ladle into deep bowls, ensuring each portion gets noodles, vegetables, and plenty of broth.
Top with the crispy Tofu cubes, fresh Cilantro, and extra lime wedges.
Add chili flakes or sriracha for heat if desired.
tips: Pressing the tofu removes excess moisture for better browning and texture.
Don't fully cook the rice noodles before adding - they'll finish in the hot broth.
Taste and adjust the fish sauce carefully - it's potent stuff.
For a vegan version, use soy sauce instead of fish sauce.

mediterranean-bowl.name: Mediterranean Grain Bowl
mediterranean-bowl.description: Quinoa bowl with hummus, falafel, and fresh vegetables

steps: Rinse the Quinoa under cold water.
Combine with water (1:2 ratio), bring to boil, reduce heat, cover and simmer for 15 minutes until fluffy.
Let rest 5 minutes with lid on.
Prepare the Falafel: if baking, preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) and bake for 15 minutes; if pan-frying, heat oil and fry until golden.
Dice the Cucumber into 1cm cubes.
Halve the Cherry Tomatoes.
Thinly slice the Red Onion.
Crumble the Feta Cheese.
Juice the Lemon.
Make the dressing: whisk together the Olive Oil, Lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Divide the cooked Quinoa among serving bowls.
Add 3 Falafel per bowl.
Create sections with diced Cucumber, halved Cherry Tomatoes, sliced Red Onion, and Kalamata Olives.
Add a generous scoop of Hummus to each bowl.
Crumble Feta Cheese over the top.
Drizzle with the lemon-olive oil dressing.
Serve with warm pita bread if desired.
tips: Rinsing quinoa is essential - the natural coating can make it taste bitter or soapy.
Baked falafel is healthier, but pan-fried gives a crispier exterior.
Let diners mix everything together at the table - the hummus becomes part of the dressing.
Add a sprinkle of za'atar or sumac for authentic Middle Eastern flavor.

sesame-crusted-tuna.name: Sesame Crusted Tuna
sesame-crusted-tuna.description: Seared ahi tuna with sesame crust and wasabi aioli

steps: Remove the Ahi Tuna Steaks from the refrigerator 20 minutes before cooking - room temperature fish sears better.
Grate the fresh Ginger.
Make the wasabi aioli by mixing the Mayonnaise with the Wasabi Paste until smooth, starting with less wasabi and adjusting to taste.
Whisk together the Soy Sauce, Rice Vinegar, and grated Ginger for the dressing.
Toss the Mixed Greens with the dressing and divide among plates.
Pat the Ahi Tuna Steaks completely dry.
Spread the Sesame Seeds on a plate and press the tuna firmly into them on all sides until completely coated.
Heat the Sesame Oil in a heavy skillet over high heat until smoking - this is crucial for a good sear.
Carefully place the tuna in the pan and sear for exactly 30-45 seconds per side for rare (the inside should remain red).
Remove immediately to a cutting board.
Slice the seared tuna against the grain into 1cm slices - it should be beautifully red inside.
Fan the tuna over the dressed greens.
Drizzle with wasabi aioli and serve immediately.
tips: Sushi-grade tuna is essential for this dish since it's served rare.
The pan must be smoking hot - a too-cool pan will cook the tuna through before getting a good sear.
Use a timer - even 10 extra seconds can overcook rare tuna.
For extra crunch, toast the sesame seeds briefly in a dry pan before coating the tuna.

butter-chicken.name: Butter Chicken
butter-chicken.description: Rich and creamy Indian chicken in tomato-butter sauce

steps: Rinse the Basmati Rice until water runs clear.
Combine with water (1:1.5 ratio), bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15 minutes.
Let rest with lid on.
Cut the Chicken Thighs into 3cm chunks.
Mix 1 tsp of Garam Masala with a pinch of Kashmiri Chili and rub over the chicken.
Finely dice the Onion.
Mince the Garlic.
Roughly chop the fresh Cilantro.
Heat half the Butter in a large heavy-bottomed pan over medium-high heat.
Add the seasoned Chicken Thighs and cook for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and slightly charred.
Transfer the chicken to a plate.
Add the remaining Butter to the same pan and sauté the Onion for 6-8 minutes until deeply golden and caramelized.
Add the minced Garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the Tomato Puree, remaining Garam Masala, and remaining Kashmiri Chili.
Simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the Heavy Cream and simmer for 2-3 minutes until the sauce is rich and velvety.
Return the chicken to the sauce and simmer for 8-10 minutes until cooked through and the flavors meld.
Warm the Naan Bread in a dry pan or under the broiler.
Fluff the Basmati Rice and divide among plates.
Ladle the creamy butter chicken over the rice.
Garnish with fresh Cilantro and serve with warm Naan Bread.
tips: Kashmiri chili provides color without intense heat - paprika is a good substitute if needed.
The butter is key - don't substitute with oil, it's what makes this dish rich and luxurious.
Letting the tomato sauce simmer before adding cream develops deeper flavor.
For restaurant-style richness, add an extra tablespoon of butter when finishing the sauce.

pesto-pasta.name: Creamy Pesto Pasta
pesto-pasta.description: Pasta tossed with fresh basil pesto and cherry tomatoes

steps: Bring a large pot of generously salted water to a rolling boil for the Penne Pasta.
Wash and thoroughly dry the Fresh Basil leaves (wet basil darkens in pesto).
Peel the Garlic cloves.
Grate the Parmesan Cheese.
Halve the Cherry Tomatoes.
Juice the Lemon.
Toast the Pine Nuts in a dry pan over medium heat for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly until golden.
Let the pine nuts cool.
In a food processor, pulse the cooled Pine Nuts and Garlic until coarsely chopped.
Add the Fresh Basil and pulse a few times.
With the processor running, slowly drizzle in the Olive Oil until smooth but still textured.
Pulse in most of the Parmesan and season with salt and pepper.
Cook the Penne Pasta in the boiling water until al dente.
Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
Combine the hot pasta with the pesto and Heavy Cream in a large bowl or the still-warm pasta pot.
Toss vigorously, adding pasta water as needed to create a creamy, clingy sauce.
Fold in the halved Cherry Tomatoes and a squeeze of Lemon juice.
Divide among warm bowls.
Top with extra grated Parmesan and whole basil leaves.
Drizzle with a little extra Olive Oil and serve immediately.
tips: Don't over-process the pesto - some texture makes it more interesting.
Adding the cream to fresh pesto makes it clingier and more luxurious.
The lemon juice brightens everything and prevents the basil from oxidizing.
Never heat pesto for too long - it can turn bitter and lose its vibrant green color.

chicken-shawarma.name: Chicken Shawarma Wraps
chicken-shawarma.description: Spiced chicken with garlic sauce in warm pita

steps: Cut the Chicken Thighs into 2cm strips.
Mix the Cumin, Coriander, Paprika, and Turmeric with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Coat the chicken thoroughly in the spice mixture and refrigerate for at least 1 hour (overnight is even better).
Mince the Garlic (half for the sauce).
Slice the Tomato.
Shred the Lettuce.
Make the garlic sauce: in a food processor, blend half the Garlic with a pinch of salt until pasty.
Very slowly drizzle in oil, alternating with lemon juice, until you have a thick, creamy sauce.
Fold in the Greek Yogurt.
Heat a grill pan or skillet over high heat.
Cook the marinated Chicken Thighs for 5-6 minutes per side until charred and cooked through (internal temp 165°F/74°C).
Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes, then slice into strips.
Warm the Pita Bread on the grill or in a dry pan until soft and pliable.
Lay out warm Pita Bread and spread garlic sauce generously on each.
Layer with shredded Lettuce and sliced Tomato.
Pile the sliced Chicken on top.
Add Pickled Turnips for color and tang.
Drizzle with more garlic sauce, fold into a wrap, and serve immediately.
tips: The longer you marinate, the more flavorful and tender the chicken becomes.
Traditional toum (garlic sauce) is made with just garlic and oil - the yogurt makes it easier and lighter.
Charring is important for authentic shawarma flavor - don't be afraid of high heat.
Pickled turnips are traditional and add a beautiful pink color plus essential acidity.

shrimp-fried-rice.name: Shrimp Fried Rice
shrimp-fried-rice.description: Classic Chinese-style fried rice with shrimp and vegetables

steps: Peel and devein the Large Shrimp if needed, patting completely dry.
Break up any clumps in the Day-old Rice - it must be cold and dry for best results.
Dice the Carrots into small 5mm cubes.
Thinly slice the Green Onions, separating whites and greens.
Mince the Garlic.
Beat the Eggs lightly.
Have all ingredients prepped and within reach before the wok goes on.
Heat a wok over high heat until smoking.
Add 1 tablespoon Vegetable Oil, pour in the beaten Eggs, let set 30 seconds, then scramble into large curds.
Transfer the scrambled eggs to a plate.
Add another tablespoon of oil and stir-fry the Large Shrimp for 2-3 minutes until pink and curled.
Transfer the shrimp to the plate with the eggs.
Add the remaining oil and stir-fry the diced Carrots for 2 minutes until slightly softened.
Add the Peas and the white parts of the Green Onions and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Add the cold Day-old Rice, breaking up any clumps, and press against the hot wok to get some char.
Push the rice to the side and add the minced Garlic to the bare wok.
Cook the garlic for 20 seconds until fragrant, then mix into the rice.
Drizzle the Soy Sauce around the edges of the wok and toss everything together.
Return the scrambled eggs and shrimp and toss to combine.
Drizzle with Sesame Oil.
Divide among plates and scatter with the green parts of the Green Onions.
Serve immediately.
tips: Day-old rice is essential - fresh rice is too moist and will become mushy. Spread fresh rice on a sheet pan and refrigerate uncovered overnight if needed.
The wok must be screaming hot throughout - this gives the smoky 'wok hei' flavor.
Adding soy sauce to the pan edges allows it to caramelize before hitting the rice.
Don't stir constantly - letting the rice sit against the hot wok creates crispy bits.

stuffed-peppers.name: Stuffed Bell Peppers
stuffed-peppers.description: Bell peppers filled with seasoned beef and rice

steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Rinse the Rice and start cooking it slightly underdone (it will finish in the oven).
Cut the tops off the Bell Peppers and remove seeds and membranes.
Finely dice the Onion.
Mince the Garlic.
Grate or shred the Mozzarella.
Roughly chop the fresh Parsley.
In a large skillet, brown the Ground Beef over medium-high heat, breaking it into small pieces.
Add the diced Onion and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Add the minced Garlic and Italian Seasoning and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in half the Canned Tomatoes and fold in the partially cooked Rice.
Season with salt and pepper.
Stand the hollowed Bell Peppers upright in a baking dish.
Spoon the beef-rice mixture into each pepper, packing gently.
Pour the remaining Canned Tomatoes around the base of the peppers.
Cover with foil and bake for 40 minutes until peppers are tender.
Remove foil, top each pepper generously with Mozzarella, and bake uncovered for 10 more minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly.
Carefully transfer each stuffed pepper to a plate.
Spoon some tomato sauce from the baking dish around each pepper.
Garnish with fresh Parsley and serve with crusty bread.
tips: If the peppers don't stand upright, trim a tiny bit off the bottom (without making a hole).
Slightly undercook the rice initially - it absorbs moisture from the tomatoes while baking.
The tomato sauce around the peppers keeps them moist and creates a built-in side dish.
For a colorful presentation, use a mix of red, yellow, and orange peppers.

orange-chicken.name: Crispy Orange Chicken
orange-chicken.description: Crispy fried chicken pieces in tangy orange glaze

steps: Rinse the Rice until water runs clear.
Combine with water in a pot, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15-18 minutes.
Cut the Chicken Breast into 3cm bite-sized pieces and toss with the Cornstarch until evenly coated.
Zest the orange, then juice it.
Mince the Garlic.
Thinly slice the Green Onions.
Make the orange sauce: whisk together the Orange Juice, Orange Zest, Soy Sauce, Brown Sugar, and Rice Vinegar until the sugar dissolves.
Heat the Vegetable Oil in a deep pot or wok to 350°F (175°C).
Working in batches to avoid crowding, carefully fry the cornstarch-coated Chicken for 4-5 minutes until golden brown, crispy, and cooked through.
Drain on a paper towel-lined plate.
Drain all but 1 tablespoon of oil from the wok and heat over medium-high.
Add the minced Garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the orange sauce and bring to a boil.
Let it reduce and thicken for 2-3 minutes until glossy and syrupy.
Add all the crispy chicken and toss vigorously to coat every piece.
Fluff the Rice and divide among plates or bowls.
Pile the glazed orange chicken on top.
Scatter with sliced Green Onions and serve immediately while the coating is still crispy.
tips: The chicken must be completely dry and evenly coated with cornstarch for crispy results.
Don't overcrowd the oil - frying in batches keeps the temperature high for crispier chicken.
Serve immediately - the sauce will soften the crispy coating over time.
For extra citrus punch, add some fresh orange zest to the finished dish.

lamb-chops.name: Herb-Crusted Lamb Chops
lamb-chops.description: Tender lamb chops with rosemary and garlic crust

steps: Remove the Lamb Chops from the refrigerator 30 minutes before cooking to bring to room temperature.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Peel and quarter the Potatoes.
Put them in a pot of salted water, bring to boil and cook for 15-20 minutes until fork-tender.
Strip the fresh Rosemary leaves and finely chop them.
Mince the Garlic finely.
Mix the Breadcrumbs with chopped Rosemary, minced Garlic, 2 tablespoons of Olive Oil, salt, and pepper.
Trim the Green Beans.
Season the Lamb Chops generously with salt and pepper.
Heat the remaining Olive Oil in an oven-safe skillet over high heat until smoking.
Sear the lamb chops for 2 minutes on each side until well browned.
Remove from heat and brush the top of each chop with Dijon Mustard.
Press the herb-breadcrumb mixture firmly onto the mustard to create a crust.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and roast for 10-12 minutes for medium-rare (internal temp 135°F/57°C).
Steam or blanch the Green Beans for 3-4 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp.
Drain the Potatoes and mash with the Butter and a splash of cream.
Season the mash with salt and pepper.
Let the Lamb Chops rest for 5 minutes - essential for juicy meat.
Divide the creamy mashed Potatoes among plates.
Arrange 2-3 lamb chops per person.
Add Green Beans alongside.
Drizzle any pan juices over the lamb.
tips: Room temperature meat is crucial for even cooking - cold lamb will be overcooked outside and raw inside.
The mustard helps the crust adhere and adds flavor without being overpowering.
Don't skip the resting time - cut too early and all the juices will run out.
For a perfect medium-rare, use an instant-read thermometer.

chicken-pot-pie.name: Classic Chicken Pot Pie
chicken-pot-pie.description: Creamy chicken and vegetables under flaky pastry

steps: Thaw the Puff Pastry according to package directions - do this first as it takes the most time.
Cut the Chicken Breast into 3cm chunks.
Bring the Chicken Stock to a simmer, add the chicken and poach for 12-15 minutes until cooked through.
Remove the chicken and shred with two forks.
Reserve the poaching stock.
Dice the Carrots.
Slice the Celery.
Dice the Onion.
Strip the Thyme leaves.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a large pot, melt the Butter over medium heat.
Add the diced Onion and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Add the diced Carrots and sliced Celery and cook for 5 minutes until slightly softened.
Sprinkle the Flour over the vegetables, stir to coat and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually pour in the reserved Chicken Stock, whisking constantly to prevent lumps.
Add the Heavy Cream and Thyme and bring to a simmer.
Cook for 5-7 minutes until the sauce thickens enough to coat a spoon.
Stir in the Peas and shredded chicken and season with salt and pepper.
Pour the filling into a baking dish.
Roll out the Puff Pastry and drape over the filling, tucking edges under or crimping against the dish.
Cut a few slits for steam to escape.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until the pastry is puffed and deeply golden brown.
Let rest 10 minutes before serving - the filling will be molten hot.
tips: Poaching the chicken in stock keeps it moist and adds flavor to the sauce.
The roux must cook for a minute before adding liquid to avoid a floury taste.
Don't skip the steam vents in the pastry - they prevent the top from getting soggy.
Brush the pastry with beaten egg before baking for an extra golden, shiny crust.

beef-tacos.name: Classic Beef Tacos
beef-tacos.description: Seasoned ground beef in crispy shells with fresh toppings

steps: Finely dice the Onion.
Shred the Lettuce into thin strips.
Dice the Tomato into small cubes.
Grate the Cheddar Cheese.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the Ground Beef and diced Onion.
Cook for 6-8 minutes, breaking the meat into small crumbles.
Drain excess fat, leaving about 1 tablespoon.
Add the Cumin, Chili Powder, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 cup water.
Stir and simmer for 3-4 minutes until liquid evaporates and spices coat the meat.
Warm the Taco Shells according to package directions (3-4 minutes in a 350°F oven or briefly in a dry skillet).
Place the seasoned beef in a serving dish alongside the toppings.
Build tacos starting with beef, then shredded Lettuce, diced Tomato, grated Cheddar Cheese, Sour Cream, and Salsa.
Serve immediately while shells are still crispy.
tips: Don't skip the water - it helps the spices bloom and creates a more flavorful, saucier meat.
Warming the taco shells makes them more pliable and less likely to crack.
For softer tacos, wrap flour tortillas in damp paper towels and microwave for 30 seconds.
For extra kick, add a diced jalapeño to the meat while cooking.

ramen-bowl.name: Pork Ramen Bowl
ramen-bowl.description: Rich pork broth with noodles, egg, and toppings

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Score the Pork Belly skin in a crosshatch pattern.
Season with salt and put it in the oven immediately - it needs 30-35 minutes.
Mince the Garlic.
Grate the fresh Ginger.
Thinly slice the Green Onions, separating whites and greens.
Drain the Corn.
Soft-boil the Eggs: bring water to a boil, gently lower in the eggs, cook for exactly 6 minutes for jammy yolks.
Transfer immediately to ice water and peel when cool.
Make the broth: combine the Chicken Stock, minced Garlic, grated Ginger, Soy Sauce, and the white parts of the Green Onions in a large pot.
Bring the broth to a simmer.
Whisk the Miso Paste with a ladleful of hot broth until smooth, then stir back into the pot.
Keep warm over low heat - don't boil the miso.
Remove the Pork Belly from the oven (it should be crispy-skinned and cooked through).
Let the pork belly rest, then slice into 1cm pieces.
Cook the Ramen Noodles according to package directions.
Drain and divide among deep bowls.
Ladle the hot miso broth generously over the noodles.
Arrange the sliced Pork Belly on one side of each bowl.
Halve the soft-boiled Eggs and place yolk-side up.
Add Corn and a sheet of Nori.
Scatter with the green parts of the Green Onions and serve immediately.
tips: The 6-minute egg is crucial - even 30 seconds more will firm up the yolk.
Never boil miso - it kills the beneficial bacteria and dulls the flavor.
The nori should be added last and eaten quickly before it gets soggy.
For extra richness, drizzle with chili oil or add a pat of butter (the 'secret' at many ramen shops).

chicken-alfredo.name: Chicken Alfredo
chicken-alfredo.description: Creamy parmesan pasta with grilled chicken

steps: Bring a large pot of generously salted water to a rolling boil for the Fettuccine.
Butterfly the Chicken Breast and pound to even thickness.
Season with Italian Seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Mince the Garlic.
Grate the Parmesan Cheese.
Roughly chop the fresh Parsley.
Cook the Fettuccine until al dente.
Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
While pasta cooks, heat a grill pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Grill the seasoned Chicken Breast for 5-6 minutes per side until charred and cooked through (internal temp 165°F/74°C).
Let rest 5 minutes.
In a large saucepan, melt the Butter over medium heat.
Add the minced Garlic and cook for 30 seconds - don't let it brown.
Pour in the Heavy Cream and bring to a gentle simmer.
Cook for 3-4 minutes until slightly reduced.
Remove from heat and stir in most of the grated Parmesan Cheese until melted and smooth.
Add the drained Fettuccine and toss to coat, adding splashes of pasta water if needed.
Season with Black Pepper.
Slice the rested Chicken Breast diagonally.
Divide the creamy Fettuccine among plates.
Arrange the sliced chicken on top.
Sprinkle with remaining Parmesan and fresh Parsley.
Finish with a generous crack of Black Pepper.
tips: Don't bring the cream to a rolling boil - gentle simmering prevents it from breaking.
Add the parmesan off the heat for the smoothest sauce - high heat can make it grainy.
The pasta water is your friend - use it to adjust sauce consistency.
This sauce is rich - don't over-sauce the pasta. Less is more.

bibimbap.name: Korean Bibimbap
bibimbap.description: Rice bowl with vegetables, beef, and gochujang sauce

steps: Rinse the Rice until water runs clear.
Combine with water, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15-18 minutes.
Keep warm.
Slice the Beef Sirloin into thin strips.
Marinate the beef with a splash of Soy Sauce and Sesame Oil.
Julienne the Carrots and Zucchini into thin matchsticks.
Wash and dry the Spinach and Bean Sprouts.
Sauté the julienned Carrots in Sesame Oil with a pinch of salt for 2 minutes and set aside.
Sauté the Zucchini the same way and set aside.
Blanch the Spinach in boiling water for 30 seconds.
Squeeze out all water and season with Sesame Oil and salt.
Blanch the Bean Sprouts for 1 minute.
Drain and season the bean sprouts with Sesame Oil and salt.
Stir-fry the marinated Beef Sirloin over high heat for 2-3 minutes until just cooked.
Set the beef aside.
Fry the Eggs sunny-side up, leaving the yolks runny.
Divide the hot Rice among large bowls.
Arrange the vegetables and beef in separate sections, creating a colorful wheel pattern.
Place a fried Egg in the center, yolk-side up.
Add a generous dollop of Gochujang on the side.
Sprinkle with Sesame Seeds.
Serve with a long spoon - diners mix everything together before eating, breaking the yolk to create a sauce.
tips: Each component is seasoned and cooked separately to maintain distinct flavors and textures.
The runny egg yolk is essential - it mixes with the gochujang to create a creamy sauce.
For extra crunch, use a stone bowl and heat it before adding rice - this creates a crispy rice crust called 'nurungji'.
Mixing is part of the experience - encourage thorough mixing for the best flavor in every bite.

eggplant-parmesan.name: Eggplant Parmesan
eggplant-parmesan.description: Breaded eggplant slices baked with marinara and cheese

steps: Slice the Eggplant into 1cm rounds.
Salt generously and let sit in a colander for 30 minutes - this draws out moisture and bitterness.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Set up the breading station: Flour in bowl 1, beaten Eggs in bowl 2, Breadcrumbs mixed with half the grated Parmesan in bowl 3.
Shred or slice the Mozzarella.
Tear the fresh Basil.
Pat the eggplant completely dry with paper towels.
Dredge each slice in Flour (shake off excess), dip in Eggs, then press into the Breadcrumb-parmesan mixture on both sides.
Heat the Olive Oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Pan-fry the breaded eggplant in batches for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown, adding more oil as needed.
Drain on paper towels.
Spread a thin layer of Marinara Sauce on the bottom of a baking dish.
Layer half the fried eggplant, overlapping slightly.
Top with 1/3 of the remaining Marinara, 1/3 of the Mozzarella, and some torn Basil.
Repeat with remaining eggplant, Marinara, Mozzarella, and Basil, ending with cheese on top.
Cover with foil and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove foil and bake another 10-15 minutes until bubbly and golden.
Let rest 10 minutes before cutting - this helps it hold together.
Serve garnished with fresh Basil and extra Parmesan, with crusty bread alongside.
tips: Don't skip the salting step - it's essential for removing bitterness and excess moisture.
Pat the eggplant very dry after salting - wet eggplant won't crisp properly.
Let the dish rest before cutting to help the layers set up.
For a lighter version, brush the breaded eggplant with oil and bake instead of frying.

kung-pao-chicken.name: Kung Pao Chicken
kung-pao-chicken.description: Spicy stir-fried chicken with peanuts and chilies

steps: Rinse the Rice until water runs clear.
Combine with water in a pot, bring to boil, reduce heat to low, cover and cook for 15-18 minutes.
Cut the Chicken Breast into 2cm cubes and toss with the Cornstarch.
Mince the Garlic and grate the fresh Ginger.
Cut the Green Onions into 3cm pieces, separating whites and greens.
Snip the Dried Chilies in half, shaking out some seeds.
Make the sauce: whisk together the Soy Sauce, Rice Vinegar, and Hoisin Sauce.
Toast the Peanuts in a dry wok over medium heat for 2-3 minutes until fragrant and golden.
Set the peanuts aside.
Heat oil in the wok over high heat until smoking.
Stir-fry the cornstarch-coated Chicken Breast in batches for 3-4 minutes until golden and cooked through.
Set the chicken aside.
Add a splash more oil and fry the Dried Chilies for 30 seconds until darkened and fragrant - don't burn them.
Add the minced Garlic, grated Ginger, and white parts of the Green Onions.
Stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Return the chicken to the wok and pour in the sauce.
Toss vigorously to coat.
Add the toasted Peanuts and green parts of the Green Onions.
Toss until everything is coated and glossy.
Fluff the Rice and divide among plates.
Spoon the Kung Pao chicken over the rice and serve immediately.
tips: The dried chilies are mainly for flavor and aroma - they're not meant to be eaten (unless you're brave).
Control the heat by removing more or fewer seeds from the chilies.
Toasting the peanuts first is essential - raw peanuts won't have the same nutty depth.
Have all ingredients ready before you start - kung pao comes together very fast.

caprese-chicken.name: Caprese Stuffed Chicken
caprese-chicken.description: Chicken breast stuffed with mozzarella, tomato, and basil

steps: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Slice the Fresh Mozzarella into thin rounds.
Slice the Roma Tomatoes into thin rounds and pat dry with paper towels.
Wash and dry the Fresh Basil leaves and Mixed Greens.
Pat the Chicken Breast dry and lay flat.
Using a sharp knife, cut a horizontal pocket into each breast, being careful not to cut all the way through.
Season the inside of each pocket with Garlic Powder, salt, and pepper.
Layer the filling inside each pocket: Fresh Mozzarella, then Roma Tomatoes, then Fresh Basil.
Secure the opening with 1-2 toothpicks and season the outside with salt and pepper.
Heat the Olive Oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the stuffed Chicken Breast for 4 minutes per side until golden brown.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake for 15-18 minutes until the chicken is cooked through (internal temp 165°F/74°C).
Let rest 5 minutes, then remove toothpicks.
Spread the Mixed Greens on a serving platter or individual plates.
Slice each stuffed chicken breast diagonally to reveal the filling.
Arrange over the greens.
Drizzle generously with Balsamic Glaze.
Garnish with remaining Fresh Basil leaves.
tips: Pat the tomato slices dry to prevent the filling from becoming watery.
Don't overstuff - the chicken needs to close around the filling.
The rest time is crucial - it lets the juices redistribute and the cheese firm up slightly for cleaner slices.
If your balsamic glaze is too thick, warm it briefly before drizzling.

beef-stroganoff.name: Beef Stroganoff
beef-stroganoff.description: Tender beef in creamy mushroom sauce over egg noodles

steps: Take the Sour Cream out of the refrigerator to come to room temperature - this prevents curdling.
Bring a large pot of salted water to boil for the Egg Noodles.
Slice the Beef Sirloin against the grain into thin 5mm strips and season with salt and pepper.
Slice the Mushrooms thickly.
Dice the Onion.
Mince the Garlic.
Roughly chop the fresh Parsley.
Cook the Egg Noodles according to package directions.
Drain and toss with a little butter to prevent sticking.
Heat half the Butter in a large skillet over high heat until smoking.
Working in batches to avoid crowding, sear the beef for 1-2 minutes until browned but still pink inside.
Transfer the beef to a plate.
Add the remaining Butter to the skillet.
Reduce heat to medium-high, add the Mushrooms and cook without stirring for 3 minutes for good browning.
Stir and cook 3 more minutes until golden.
Add the Onion and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Add the Garlic and cook 30 seconds.
Pour in the Beef Stock and Dijon Mustard.
Bring to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes until slightly reduced.
Remove from heat and stir in the room-temperature Sour Cream until smooth.
Return the beef and any accumulated juices and gently stir to combine - don't boil or the sour cream will curdle.
Divide the buttered Egg Noodles among warm plates.
Spoon the creamy beef stroganoff over the noodles.
Garnish generously with fresh Parsley and serve immediately.
tips: Don't overcook the beef - it should be pink in the middle after searing, as it will finish cooking in the sauce.
Room temperature sour cream is essential to prevent curdling - take it out of the fridge 30 minutes before cooking.
Don't boil the sauce after adding sour cream - just warm it gently.
The Dijon mustard adds depth without making the dish taste like mustard.

cheesy-pork-quesadillas.name: Cheesy Pork Quesadillas
cheesy-pork-quesadillas.description: Crispy tortillas stuffed with seasoned pork, melted cheese, and peppers

steps: Finely dice the Onion and Bell Pepper.
Grate the Cheddar Cheese and Monterey Jack, then mix together.
Roughly chop the fresh Cilantro.
