Mix the Cumin and Chili Powder with a pinch of salt.
Heat the Vegetable Oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the Ground Pork, breaking into small crumbles, and cook for 4-5 minutes until starting to brown.
Add the diced Onion and Bell Pepper and cook for 4-5 minutes until vegetables are softened and pork is cooked through.
Sprinkle with the spice mixture and cook for 1 more minute.
Transfer to a bowl.
Wipe out the skillet and return to medium heat.
Place one Flour Tortilla in the pan.
Spread a generous handful of mixed cheese on one half, spoon some pork filling over the cheese, then fold in half.
Cook for 2-3 minutes until the bottom is golden and crispy.
Carefully flip and cook another 2 minutes until cheese is fully melted.
Transfer to a cutting board and repeat with remaining tortillas.
Cut each quesadilla into 3 wedges.
Arrange on a platter and serve with Sour Cream and Salsa for dipping.
Garnish with fresh Cilantro.
tips: Don't overfill the quesadillas - too much filling makes them hard to flip and the cheese won't hold everything together.
Medium heat is key - too hot and the tortilla burns before the cheese melts.
Press gently with a spatula while cooking to help the cheese melt and bind everything.
For extra crispy quesadillas, brush the outside of the tortilla with a little oil before cooking.

greek-beef-gnocchi.name: Greek-Inspired Beef Gnocchi Skillet
greek-beef-gnocchi.description: Pan-fried gnocchi with seasoned beef, feta, and Mediterranean flavors

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil for the Potato Gnocchi.
Halve the Cherry Tomatoes.
Roughly chop the Kalamata Olives.
Thinly slice the Red Onion.
Mince the Garlic.
Crumble the Feta Cheese.
Zest and juice the Lemon.
Cook the Potato Gnocchi in the boiling water until they float.
Drain well.
Heat 2 tablespoons of Olive Oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the cooked gnocchi in a single layer and cook without stirring for 3-4 minutes until golden and crispy on the bottom.
Toss and cook another 2 minutes, then transfer to a plate.
Add the remaining Olive Oil to the skillet.
Add the Ground Beef and cook for 5-6 minutes, breaking into crumbles, until browned.
Add the sliced Red Onion and cook for 2-3 minutes until softened.
Add the minced Garlic and Oregano and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the halved Cherry Tomatoes and chopped Kalamata Olives and cook for 2-3 minutes until tomatoes soften and release their juices.
Return the crispy gnocchi to the pan.
Add the Spinach in handfuls, tossing until wilted.
Squeeze the Lemon juice over everything and toss to combine.
Divide among plates.
Crumble Feta Cheese generously over the top.
Sprinkle with Lemon zest and a drizzle of extra Olive Oil.
Serve immediately while the gnocchi is still crispy.
tips: Don't skip pan-frying the gnocchi - it transforms them from soft pillows to crispy, golden bites.
Let the gnocchi sit undisturbed in the pan to develop a proper crust.
Add the spinach last so it just wilts and stays vibrant green.
The lemon juice at the end brightens all the Mediterranean flavors.

butternut-squash-orzotto.name: Butternut Squash and Sage Orzotto
butternut-squash-orzotto.description: Creamy orzo cooked risotto-style with roasted squash and crispy sage

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Peel the Butternut Squash and cut into 2cm cubes.
Toss with the Olive Oil, salt, and pepper and roast immediately - it needs 25-30 minutes.
Finely dice the Onion.
Mince the Garlic.
Finely grate the Parmesan Cheese.
Pick the Fresh Sage leaves from their stems.
Heat the Vegetable Stock in a saucepan and keep warm over low heat.
Melt half the Butter in a large, deep skillet over medium heat.
Add the Fresh Sage leaves and fry for 1-2 minutes until crispy and the butter is lightly browned.
Transfer sage to a paper towel, leaving the browned butter in the pan.
Add the diced Onion to the sage butter and cook for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Add the minced Garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the Orzo Pasta and stir for 2 minutes until lightly toasted and coated in butter.
Pour in the White Wine and stir until absorbed.
Add the warm Vegetable Stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently, waiting until each addition is mostly absorbed before adding the next.
Continue for 15-18 minutes until orzo is al dente and creamy.
Remove from heat and stir in the remaining Butter, grated Parmesan, and Nutmeg.
Gently fold in most of the roasted Butternut Squash, reserving some for garnish.
Divide among warm bowls.
Top with the reserved roasted Butternut Squash.
Crumble the crispy Fresh Sage over each portion.
Finish with extra Parmesan and a crack of black pepper.
tips: Keep the stock warm - adding cold liquid will slow cooking and affect the creamy texture.
Orzotto is more forgiving than risotto - you don't need to stir constantly, just frequently.
The crispy sage is a game-changer - don't skip this step.
For extra richness, stir in a splash of cream with the parmesan.

bacon-mac-and-cheese.name: Creamy Bacon Mac and Cheese
bacon-mac-and-cheese.description: Ultimate comfort food with crispy bacon and a rich three-cheese sauce

steps: Take the Whole Milk out of the refrigerator to come to room temperature - this makes a smoother sauce.
Bring a large pot of salted water to boil for the Elbow Macaroni.
Cut the Bacon into 1cm pieces.
Grate all three cheeses.
Finely chop the fresh Chives.
Cook the Elbow Macaroni until just al dente (1 minute less than package directions).
Drain.
Cook the Bacon pieces over medium heat for 6-8 minutes until crispy.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate, leaving the bacon fat in the pan.
Reduce heat to medium-low, add the Butter to the bacon fat and melt.
Whisk in the Flour and cook for 1-2 minutes until slightly golden and nutty-smelling.
Gradually pour in the room-temperature Whole Milk, whisking constantly.
Continue for 5-7 minutes until the sauce thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon.
Remove from heat and stir in the Dijon Mustard, Garlic Powder, and a pinch of Paprika.
Add the grated cheeses in three additions, stirring until each is fully melted into a silky smooth sauce.
Fold in the cooked Elbow Macaroni and most of the crispy Bacon (reserve some for topping).
Divide among bowls.
Top with reserved crispy Bacon, fresh Chives, and a dusting of Paprika.
Serve immediately.
tips: Undercook the pasta slightly - it will continue cooking in the hot cheese sauce.
Low heat is essential when adding cheese - too hot and it becomes grainy instead of smooth.
The combination of cheddar (flavor), gruyere (meltiness), and parmesan (sharpness) creates the ultimate cheese sauce.
For a baked version, transfer to a baking dish, top with breadcrumbs and extra cheese, and broil until golden.

teppanyaki-beef-udon.name: Teppanyaki-Style Beef Udon
teppanyaki-beef-udon.description: Thick udon noodles stir-fried with shaved beef, mushrooms, and vegetables in honey-garlic teriyaki sauce

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the Udon Noodles.
Drain the Shaved Beef and pat very dry with paper towels.
Thinly slice the Mushrooms.
Shred the Cabbage into thin strips.
Julienne the Carrots into thin matchsticks.
Thinly slice the Green Onions.
Mince the Garlic and grate the fresh Ginger.
Make the sauce: whisk together the Honey, Soy Sauce, and Oyster Sauce.
Cook the Udon Noodles for 1-3 minutes until tender but still firm.
Drain, rinse with warm water, and toss with a drizzle of Vegetable Oil to prevent sticking.
Heat a large non-stick pan or wok over high heat.
Add 1 tablespoon Vegetable Oil and the Mushrooms, Cabbage, Carrots, and the Garlic-Ginger mixture.
Season with salt and pepper and stir-fry for 2-4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Transfer the vegetables to a plate.
Return the pan to medium-high heat and add the Butter, letting it melt and foam.
Add the Shaved Beef, separating the layers, and cook for 1-3 minutes, stirring often, until just cooked through - don't overcook.
Add the udon noodles, cooked vegetables, Spinach, and the sauce mixture to the pan.
Toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until the spinach wilts and everything is glossy.
Divide among large bowls.
Scatter with sliced Green Onions.
Sprinkle generously with Sesame Seeds and serve immediately.
tips: Shaved Beef cooks very quickly - have everything else ready before you start cooking the meat.
Patting the beef dry is essential - wet meat will steam instead of sear.
Fresh or frozen Udon Noodles work best - they have the perfect chewy texture.
The Butter adds richness that makes this dish special - don't substitute with oil.

crispy-beef-taquitos.name: Crispy Beef Taquitos
crispy-beef-taquitos.description: Rolled tortillas filled with seasoned beef and cheese, baked until golden

steps: Take the Cream Cheese out of the refrigerator to soften at room temperature.
Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Finely dice the Onion.
Mince the Garlic.
Grate the Cheddar Cheese.
Roughly chop the fresh Cilantro.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the Ground Beef and cook for 5-6 minutes, breaking into crumbles, until browned.
Add the diced Onion and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the minced Garlic, Cumin, and Chili Powder and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Reduce heat to low and stir in the softened Cream Cheese until fully melted and the mixture is creamy.
Season with salt and pepper and remove from heat.
Warm the Flour Tortillas in the microwave for 20 seconds (covered with a damp paper towel) to make them pliable.
Place 2-3 tablespoons of beef filling along the edge of each tortilla.
Sprinkle with Cheddar Cheese.
Roll each tortilla tightly and place seam-side down on the baking sheet.
Brush tops generously with Olive Oil.
Bake for 15-18 minutes until golden brown and crispy, flipping halfway through.
Arrange on a platter and serve with Salsa and Sour Cream for dipping.
Garnish with fresh Cilantro.
tips: The cream cheese is the secret - it makes the filling creamy and helps bind everything together.
Warming the tortillas is essential - cold tortillas will crack when rolled.
Roll them tight! Loose taquitos will unroll during baking.
For air fryer: cook at 400°F for 8-10 minutes, flipping halfway through.

french-onion-beef-noodles.name: French Onion Beef Noodles
french-onion-beef-noodles.description: Tender beef and egg noodles in a rich caramelized onion and mushroom white wine sauce

steps: Slice the Onions into thin half-moons.
Melt half the Butter in a large, deep skillet over medium heat.
Add the Onions with a pinch of salt and caramelize for 25-30 minutes, stirring occasionally - low and slow is key.
Slice the Mushrooms thickly.
Slice the Beef Sirloin against the grain into thin strips.
Mince the Garlic.
Grate the Gruyere Cheese.
Roughly chop the fresh Parsley.
When onions are deeply caramelized and jammy, push them to the side and increase heat to high.
Add the Beef Sirloin strips and sear for 2 minutes until browned.
Transfer beef and onions to a plate.
Add the remaining Butter and sauté the Mushrooms for 5-6 minutes until golden brown.
Add the minced Garlic and Thyme and cook for 30 seconds.
Pour in the White Wine, scraping up browned bits.
Let reduce by half, about 2 minutes.
Add the Beef Stock and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the Heavy Cream and simmer 2-3 more minutes until the sauce coats a spoon.
Cook the Egg Noodles according to package directions.
Drain well.
Return the caramelized onions and beef to the pan.
Add the drained noodles and toss to coat everything in the sauce.
Top generously with grated Gruyere Cheese.
For extra indulgence, broil for 2 minutes until bubbly and golden.
Garnish with fresh Parsley and serve immediately.
tips: Caramelizing onions properly takes time - at least 25 minutes. This is where all the flavor comes from.
Use a good drinking wine - if you wouldn't drink it, don't cook with it.
The gruyere adds nuttiness and melts beautifully - don't substitute with cheddar.
For a soup-ier version, add more beef stock and serve in deep bowls with crusty bread.

asian-sesame-chicken-salad.name: Asian Sesame Chicken Salad
asian-sesame-chicken-salad.description: Crispy chicken over crunchy vegetables with sesame-ginger dressing

steps: Pound the Chicken Breast to even thickness and season with salt and pepper.
Finely shred the Red Cabbage.
Julienne the Carrots into thin matchsticks.
Drain the Mandarin Oranges.
Grate the fresh Ginger.
Make the sesame dressing: whisk together the Sesame Oil, Rice Vinegar, Soy Sauce, Honey, and grated Ginger until emulsified.
Heat a grill pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook the Chicken Breast for 5-6 minutes per side until charred and cooked through (internal temp 165°F/74°C).
While chicken cooks, blanch the Edamame in boiling water for 2-3 minutes.
Drain and cool the edamame.
Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes, then slice into strips.
In a large bowl, combine the Mixed Greens, shredded Red Cabbage, julienned Carrots, and Edamame.
Drizzle with most of the dressing and toss gently to coat.
Divide among plates.
Arrange the sliced chicken on top.
Scatter with Mandarin Oranges and crispy Wonton Strips.
Sprinkle generously with Sesame Seeds.
Drizzle with remaining dressing.
tips: Let the chicken rest before slicing to keep it juicy.
Add the wonton strips just before serving to keep them crispy.
For meal prep, keep the dressing separate until ready to eat.
Swap chicken for crispy tofu for a vegetarian version.

spinach-strawberry-salad.name: Spinach and Strawberry Salad
spinach-strawberry-salad.description: Fresh spinach with sweet strawberries, pecans, and tangy poppy seed dressing

steps: Toast the Pecans in a dry skillet over medium heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring frequently, until fragrant and slightly darkened.
Let cool, then roughly chop.
Make the poppy seed dressing: combine the Olive Oil, Balsamic Vinegar, Honey, Dijon Mustard, and Poppy Seeds in a jar or bowl and shake or whisk until emulsified.
Season with salt and pepper.
Wash and thoroughly dry the Baby Spinach - wet leaves will make the salad soggy.
Hull the Strawberries and slice into quarters.
Thinly slice the Red Onion.
Crumble the Goat Cheese.
Place the Baby Spinach in a large salad bowl or on individual plates.
Drizzle with most of the dressing and toss gently.
Scatter the sliced Strawberries, sliced Red Onion, and toasted Pecans over the spinach.
Crumble the Goat Cheese over the top.
Drizzle with remaining dressing and serve immediately.
tips: The key to this salad is contrast - sweet berries, tangy cheese, crunchy nuts.
Toast the pecans fresh - pre-toasted nuts from the store won't have the same flavor.
If strawberries aren't in season, use fresh raspberries or blackberries.
Add grilled chicken to make this a complete meal.

classic-cobb-salad.name: Classic Cobb Salad
classic-cobb-salad.description: Rows of chicken, bacon, eggs, avocado, and blue cheese over crisp greens

steps: Place the Eggs in a pot of cold water, bring to a boil, cover and remove from heat.
Let sit for 10 minutes, then transfer to ice water - start this first as eggs take the most passive time.
Season the Chicken Breast with salt and pepper.
Chop the Romaine Lettuce into bite-sized pieces.
Halve the Cherry Tomatoes.
Crumble the Blue Cheese.
Mince the Garlic.
Finely chop the Chives.
Make the dressing: whisk together the Red Wine Vinegar, Olive Oil, Dijon Mustard, minced Garlic, salt, and pepper until emulsified.
Dice the Avocado and toss with a little lemon juice to prevent browning.
Peel and quarter the cooled hard-boiled Eggs.
Cook the Bacon in a skillet over medium heat for 6-8 minutes until crispy.
Drain, then crumble or chop the bacon.
In the same skillet over medium-high heat, cook the Chicken Breast for 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through.
Let rest 5 minutes, then slice.
Spread the chopped Romaine on a large platter.
Arrange all toppings in neat rows: sliced Chicken, crumbled Bacon, quartered Eggs, diced Avocado, halved Cherry Tomatoes, and crumbled Blue Cheese.
Sprinkle with fresh Chives.
Drizzle with dressing or serve on the side.
Toss at the table.
tips: The presentation is key - arrange ingredients in distinct rows for the classic Cobb look.
Use the bacon fat to cook the chicken for extra flavor.
If you don't like blue cheese, substitute with feta or goat cheese.
This salad is a meal - don't skimp on the protein toppings.

carbonara-pasta.name: Spaghetti Carbonara
carbonara-pasta.description: Classic Roman pasta with a silky egg and Parmesan sauce, crispy bacon, and a bold hit of black pepper

steps: Remove the Eggs from the refrigerator 30 minutes before cooking - room-temperature eggs blend more smoothly into a creamy sauce.
Bring a large pot of generously salted water to a rolling boil.
Whisk the Eggs with half the grated Parmesan Cheese and season very generously with Black Pepper - this is your sauce.
Cut the Bacon into small lardons.
Slice the Onion thinly.
Mince the Garlic finely.
Grate the remaining Parmesan.
Add the Spaghetti to the boiling water and cook until al dente - about 1 minute less than package directions.
While pasta cooks, place the Bacon lardons in a cold, dry skillet and set over medium heat.
Render slowly for 6-8 minutes until crispy and golden.
Add the sliced Onion and cook for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add the minced Garlic, stir 30 seconds, then remove the pan from the heat.
Reserve at least 1 cup of starchy pasta water.
Drain the Spaghetti and immediately transfer it to the bacon pan.
Toss vigorously to coat every strand in the rendered fat.
Working quickly off the heat, pour the egg-cheese sauce over the pasta, tossing constantly.
The residual heat cooks the eggs gently into a silky, creamy sauce - never a scramble.
Add reserved pasta water a small splash at a time, tossing continuously, until the sauce is fluid, glossy, and clings to the pasta.
Twist the carbonara into nests in warm, deep bowls.
Top with remaining Parmesan and a very generous crack of fresh Black Pepper.
Serve immediately - the sauce tightens as it cools.
tips: The golden rule of carbonara: no cream, ever. The creaminess comes entirely from the emulsification of eggs, cheese, fat, and starchy pasta water.
Heat is the enemy — always remove the pan from the burner before adding the egg mixture, and keep tossing to prevent scrambling.
Use the pasta water generously. The dissolved starch acts as an emulsifier that keeps the sauce silky and stops it from breaking.
Authentic Roman carbonara uses Guanciale (cured pork cheek) and Pecorino Romano. Bacon and Parmesan are excellent and widely available substitutes.

garlic-butter-beef-fried-rice.name: Garlic Butter Beef Fried Rice
garlic-butter-beef-fried-rice.description: High-heat wok fried rice with seared sirloin strips, golden garlic butter, and a rich umami glaze

steps: If cooking rice fresh, spread it on a sheet tray and refrigerate uncovered for at least 1 hour - cold, dry Day-old Rice is essential as freshly cooked rice is too wet.
Thinly slice the Beef Sirloin against the grain into 3mm strips.
Toss with 1 tbsp Soy Sauce and Black Pepper and marinate for 10 minutes.
Finely mince all the Garlic.
Finely dice the Carrots.
Slice the white parts of the Green Onions into 1cm rounds and the green tops thinly on a bias.
Stir together the remaining Soy Sauce and Oyster Sauce in a small bowl.
Heat a wok or large cast-iron skillet over the highest possible heat until visibly smoking.
Add a thin layer of oil and the marinated Beef Sirloin in a single, uncrowded layer.
Sear without stirring for 1-2 minutes until deeply caramelized underneath.
Flip and cook 30-45 seconds more - beef should still be slightly pink inside.
Transfer to a plate.
Reduce heat to medium-high and add the Butter, letting it foam and turn golden.
Add the minced Garlic and white parts of the Green Onions.
Stir constantly for 1-2 minutes until garlic is deeply golden - watch carefully to prevent burning.
Add the diced Carrots and Peas and stir-fry for 2 minutes until carrots are just tender.
Push vegetables to the edges and crack the Eggs into the center.
Scramble briskly until just barely set.
Add the Day-old Rice, breaking up any clumps, and stir-fry on high heat for 3-4 minutes, pressing rice against the hot surface to toast it.
Pour the soy and oyster sauce mixture over the rice.
Toss continuously for 1 minute until every grain is coated and glossy.
Return the seared Beef Sirloin and any resting juices.
Drizzle the Sesame Oil over everything and toss to combine.
Transfer to a serving platter or individual bowls.
Scatter thinly sliced green parts of the Green Onions generously over the top.
Serve immediately - fried rice is best eaten straight from the wok.
tips: Day-old rice is not optional — it is the single most important factor for non-sticky, perfectly textured fried rice.
Maximum heat is the second rule. If your wok isn't smoking, the rice will steam instead of fry. Cook in batches if your burner isn't powerful enough.
Sear the beef in a single uncrowded layer — piling it up traps steam and prevents the crust from forming.
The garlic butter is the secret weapon. Let it go a shade darker than you think is safe — that deep golden garlic is what makes this recipe irresistible.

galette-bretonne.name: Breton Buckwheat Galette
galette-bretonne.description: Traditional Breton buckwheat galette filled with ham, cheese and egg

steps: Combine the Buckwheat Flour and Salt in a large mixing bowl and make a well in the center.
Crack one of the Eggs into the well and begin incorporating the flour from the edges.
Gradually pour in the Water while whisking continuously until the batter is smooth and lump-free.
Melt 30g of the Butter and whisk it into the batter.
Cover and refrigerate the batter for at least 1 hour — or ideally overnight — to allow the buckwheat to fully hydrate.
Remove the batter from the refrigerator and whisk to recombine; if it is too thick, stir in a splash of cold water until it coats the back of a spoon.
Slice the Ham into thin strips, grate the Gruyere Cheese, and crack the remaining Eggs one by one into individual small bowls.
Heat a crêpe pan or flat skillet over medium-high heat for 2 minutes until very hot.
Brush the pan with a thin layer of the remaining Butter.
Pour approximately 80ml of batter into the center and immediately tilt the pan in a circular motion to spread it into a thin, even round about 30cm in diameter.
Cook over medium heat for 2-3 minutes until the edges look dry and slightly crispy and the underside is golden brown.
Flip the galette and cook for 30-45 seconds on the second side.
Scatter a portion of grated Gruyere Cheese over the center, lay a strip of Ham on top, then slide one of the cracked Eggs into the center.
Fold the four sides of the galette inward to form a square, leaving the egg yolk visible in the center.
Cover the pan and cook over medium-low heat for 2-3 minutes until the egg white is just set but the yolk remains runny.
Slide the galette onto a warm plate and serve immediately.
Repeat for the remaining galettes, brushing the pan with Butter between each one.
tips: Resting the batter is non-negotiable — buckwheat flour needs time to fully hydrate, and a rested batter spreads more evenly and produces crispier edges.
Authentic Breton galettes use pure buckwheat flour and no wheat; if buckwheat is hard to find, a blend of 80% buckwheat and 20% wheat flour makes a more forgiving batter.
Galettes freeze beautifully — stack them between sheets of parchment paper, wrap well, and freeze for up to 2 months; reheat directly in a hot dry pan for 1-2 minutes per side.
For the full Breton experience, serve with a chilled bowl of brut Breton cider and follow with sweet crêpes for dessert.

crepe-bretonne.name: Sweet Breton Crêpe
crepe-bretonne.description: Classic sweet Breton crêpe with salted butter and vanilla

steps: Sift the Wheat Flour and Salt into a large mixing bowl and make a well in the center.
Add the Eggs and Sugar to the well and whisk from the center outward, gradually incorporating the flour.
Slowly pour in the Whole Milk while whisking continuously until the batter is completely smooth and free of lumps.
Melt 40g of the Salted Butter, let it cool slightly, then whisk it into the batter along with the Vanilla Extract.
Cover and rest the batter at room temperature for 30 minutes — this relaxes the gluten for more tender crêpes.
After resting, whisk the batter once more; it should be the consistency of thin cream — add a splash of milk if needed.
Heat a crêpe pan or non-stick skillet over medium-high heat for 1-2 minutes until hot.
Rub the remaining Salted Butter onto the pan using a folded paper towel.
Pour approximately 60ml of batter into the center and immediately tilt the pan in a circular motion to spread it thinly and evenly.
Cook over medium heat for 1-2 minutes until the edges curl slightly and the underside is lightly golden.
Flip with a thin spatula and cook the second side for 30-45 seconds until pale golden.
Slide the crêpe onto a warm plate and cover loosely with a clean kitchen towel to keep warm and moist.
Repeat with the remaining batter, stacking crêpes on top of each other — the stack stays warm and the crêpes stay supple.
Spread each warm crêpe generously with softened Salted Butter, sprinkle with Sugar, fold into quarters, and serve immediately.
For a classic Breton presentation, arrange the folded crêpes on a platter with a small bowl of extra melted Salted Butter on the side.
tips: Resting the batter is essential — a rested batter produces thinner, more tender crêpes that roll and fold without tearing.
In Brittany, salted butter (beurre salé or beurre demi-sel) is the defining ingredient — never substitute unsalted butter, as that sweet-salty balance is the soul of the Breton crêpe.
Crêpes keep well: stack on a plate, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for up to 3 days, or freeze for up to 2 months with a sheet of parchment between each one.
For crêpe au caramel au beurre salé, drizzle warm salted caramel over the crêpe, fold in quarters, and finish with a knob of salted butter for an iconic Breton dessert.
